Class TransactionDAO

java.lang.Object
Database.TransactionDAO
public class TransactionDAO extends Object
Data Access Object (DAO) for managing expense transaction records in the database.

Provides full CRUD operations for expenses: saving new records, retrieving all or filtered expenses, updating existing entries, and deleting records by ID.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    TransactionDAO()
    Constructs a new TransactionDAO for expense persistence operations.

  • Constructor Details

    • TransactionDAO

      public TransactionDAO()
      Constructs a new TransactionDAO for expense persistence operations.

  • Method Details

    • saveExpense

      public boolean saveExpense(Expense expense)
      Inserts a new expense record into the database using today's date.
      Parameters:
      expense - the Expense to persist (must have amount, categoryId, notes, cycleId, and userPin set)
      Returns:
      true if the record was inserted successfully; false otherwise

    • getAllExpenses

      public List<Expense> getAllExpenses()
      Retrieves all expense records from the database, ordered by date descending.
      Returns:
      a list of all Expense objects; empty list if none exist

    • getFilteredExpenses

      public List<Expense> getFilteredExpenses(int categoryID, String startDate, String endDate)
      Retrieves a filtered list of expenses based on category and date range.

      If categoryID is 0, all categories are included.

      Parameters:
      categoryID - the category ID to filter by; use 0 for all categories
      startDate - the start date string in yyyy-MM-dd format (inclusive)
      endDate - the end date string in yyyy-MM-dd format (inclusive)
      Returns:
      a filtered list of Expense objects; empty list if none match

    • updateExpense

      public boolean updateExpense(int id, double amount, int catId, String notes)
      Updates the amount, category, and notes of an existing expense by its ID.
      Parameters:
      id - the ID of the expense to update
      amount - the new expense amount
      catId - the new category ID
      notes - the new notes string
      Returns:
      true if at least one row was updated; false otherwise

    • deleteExpense

      public boolean deleteExpense(int id)
      Deletes an expense record from the database by its ID.
      Parameters:
      id - the ID of the expense to delete
      Returns:
      true if the record was deleted; false otherwise

    • mapResultSetToExpense

      private Expense mapResultSetToExpense(ResultSet rs) throws SQLException
      Maps a database ResultSet row to an Expense object. Falls back to the current date if the stored date string cannot be parsed.
      Parameters:
      rs - the ResultSet positioned at the row to map
      Returns:
      the populated Expense object
      Throws:
      SQLException - if a database access error occurs
